name: Kolachky
recipeInstructions:
Dissolve yeast in lukewarm water. Scald milk and set aside until lukewarm, not cold. Cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Add salt and mix again. Mix dissolved yeast with milk. Gradually add 5 cups flour and beat well. Stir in the creamed mixture and beat again. Then gently fold in eggs and blend. Continue to incorporate remaining ingredients gradually by adding additional flour a tablespoon at a time until dough is smooth enough to handle, then knead on a warmed board. The dough should be smooth and elastic, not hard or stiff. Place in a greased bowl, cover, and allow to rise in a warm place about 2.5 hours until doubled in bulk.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). On a floured warmed surface, roll dough to about 1/4 inch thickness. Cut into 2-inch squares. Add a small amount of filling (recipe below) onto each square and carefully fold the four corners over each other, pressing together so that the folded dough is thinner than the dough beneath the filling. When properly folded, the filling should be visible through all four sides. Arrange on a large baking sheet about 2 inches apart. Let rise in a warm place until doubled, approximately 45 minutes. Bake for 25 minutes at 375°F (190°C). Sprinkle generously with powdered sugar once cooled. Yields about six dozen cakes.
PRUNE FILLING:
Wash and cook prunes in enough water to cover. Boil until tender, then drain, remove pits, and chop finely. Sweeten and add cinnamon or orange peel to taste. Allow to cool slightly before spreading.
POPPY SEED FILLING:
Combine ingredients in a saucepan over medium heat. Cook for about five minutes until thickened.
APPLE FILLING:
In a skillet, cook apples with cinnamon candies until softened, about five to six minutes. Remove from heat and let cool.
